macro_segments.title: Introduction to Lishmah and the Case of Writing a Get for One of Two Wives with the Same Name

micro_segments.title: Review of Previous Discussion: The Requirement of Lishmah and the Problem of Identical Names
The Gemara's Question: Does the Identity of the Wife Holding the Get Clarify Its Validity
Rabbi Eliezer's Position: Shtar Re'ayah Not Essential; Adei Mesirah Sufficient

macro_segments.title: Rei'ach Haget: The Doctrine of Invalid Gets That Appear Valid and Create Liminal Status

micro_segments.title: Rav's Position: All These Gets Are Invalid for Kehunah Except the First (Written as Practice Only)
The Concept of Rei'ach Haget: When a Get Smells Valid but Is Invalid, Rendering a Woman Forbidden to Kohanim
Shmuel's Disagreement: All Invalid Gets Create the Rei'ach Haget Problem
Shmuel's Consistent Position on Pasul: Invalid Yet Creates Forbidden Status
Rambam's Distinction: Pasul (Rabbinically Invalid) versus Batel (Scripturally Void)

macro_segments.title: The Geographical and Doctrinal Dispute: Babylonian Amoraim vs. Land of Israel Sages on Rei'ach Haget

micro_segments.title: In Marava: Rabbi Eliezer on Chalitzah: Left Foot or Night Render It Pasul U'Poselet
The Contrast: Obvious Problems (Katan, Anfilya) Are Pasul But Do Not Disqualify; Subtle Problems Disqualify
Zeiri's Narrow Position: Only the Last Case (Two Wives, Same Name) Creates Rei'ach Haget
Rav Assi Agrees with Zeiri: Only the Last Case Is Rei'ach Haget; Others Are Not
Rabbi Yochanan's Radical Position: No Get Is Rei'ach Haget; All Invalid Gets Are Simply Invalid

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Consistency with Ein Breira: Inheritance, Chalitzah, and the Last Mishnah Case

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Principle: Ein Breira (No Retroactive Determination); Brothers Who Divide Are Purchasers
Yovel and Inheritance: Because Inheritance Is Not a Sale, Breira Does Not Apply the Same Way
Resolving the Apparent Contradiction: Lishmah Is a Special Requirement; Breira Debate Stands Separate

macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Breira Sugya: The Case of Two Wives and the Difference Between Toleh Bedaat Atzmo and Toleh Bedaat Acherim

micro_segments.title: Rav Hoshaya's Question: Does Toleh Bedaat Acherim (Write for the Wife Who Walks Out First) Work?
The Distinction: Toleh Bedaat Atzmo (Indecisive, You Decide Later) vs Toleh Bedaat Acherim (You've Decided, Others Act)
Rashi's Explanation: Toleh Bedaat Atzmo Shows Indecisiveness (Pose'ach Al Shtei Hasefim); Cannot Support Breira

macro_segments.title: The Pesach Korban Proof: When the First Son Enters Jerusalem, He and His Brothers Retroactively Become Included

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: A Father's Conditional Offer to Slaughter Pesach for the First Son to Arrive in Jerusalem
The Consequence: The First Son Who Enters Retroactively Becomes Included, and Through Him, All Brothers
Rabbi Yochanan's Response: This Is Not True Breira; It Is Zerizus (Alacrity), Not Determination
Proof from the Baraita: Daughters Arrived First; Yet Sons Were Still Part of the Korban

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Critique and the Question of Whether Toleh Bedaat Acherim Can Succeed for a Get

micro_segments.title: Abaye Points Out the Respondent Missed the Distinction: Toleh Bedaat Atzmo vs Toleh Bedaat Acherim Are Different
Abaye's Question Stands: The Pesach Korban Case Suggests Toleh Bedaat Acherim Might Work

macro_segments.title: Rav Mesharshiya's Proof: Rabbi Yehuda Holds Toleh Bedaat Atzmo Has No Breira; Toleh Bedaat Acherim Does

micro_segments.title: Evidence from Wine Separation: Rabbi Yehuda Says You Cannot Separate Trumot and Ma'asrot for a Time TBD (Toleh Bedaat Atzmo)
Rabbi Meir vs Rabbi Yehuda on Wine: Meir Allows Breira; Yehuda, Yose, and Shimon Do Not
Evidence from Conditional Get: If He Does Not Recover from Illness (Toleh Bedaat Acherim—Dependent on God/Others)
The Get Takes Effect a Minute Before Death: That Time Is TBD, Yet This Is Breira That Works

macro_segments.title: Second Proof from Rav Mesharshiya: Rabbi Shimon and Kiddushei Biah with Father's Condition

micro_segments.title: The Case: A Man Says to a Woman, 'Our Sex Is Kiddushin If Your Father Agrees' (Toleh Bedaat Acherim)
Rabbi Shimon's Position: If Father Agrees, She Is Mekudeshet; If Not, She Is Not
Why This Is Breira, Not Tanai: Kiddushei Biah Creates Marriage by Marital Context, Not by Condition

macro_segments.title: Rava's Counter-Argument: No Distinction Between Toleh Bedaat Atzmo and Toleh Bedaat Acherim; Everyone Holds of Breira

micro_segments.title: Rava's Position: Both Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbi Shimon Hold of Breira Across the Board
Why the Wine Case Fails: Not Because Breira Doesn't Work, But Because of Risk That Wine Spills
Rabbi Meir's Response: Even If Wine Spills, I Am Not Concerned
Rava's Resolution: The Others Agree in Principle That Breira Works; They Just Impose a Practical Safeguard

topical_tags.term: Lishmah (Intent at Time of Writing)
Rei'ach Haget (Get Smells Valid but Is Invalid)
Pasul (Rabbinically Invalid)
Shtar Re'ayah (Document as Evidence)
Adei Mesirah (Witnesses to Delivery)
Grusha (Divorced Woman; Forbidden to Kohanim)
Rav (Babylonian Amora; Restrictive on Rei'ach Haget)
Shmuel (Babylonian Amora; Expansive on Rei'ach Haget)
Rabbi Eliezer (Tanna; Associated with Restrictive View)
Zeiri (Amora; Narrow View of Rei'ach Haget)
Rav Assi (Amora; Agrees with Zeiri)
Rabbi Yochanan (Amora; Rejects Rei'ach Haget Entirely)
Ein Breira (No Retroactive Determination)
Yesh Breira (Retroactive Determination Possible)
Toleh Bedaat Atzmo (Indecisive; You Decide Later)
Toleh Bedaat Acherim (You Decided; Others Act)
Pose'ach Al Shtei Hasefim (Jumping Between Two Doorsteps; Indecision)
Pesach Korban (Passover Sacrifice; Case Where Breira May Work)
Rav Hoshaya (Amora; Posed Question on Breira)
Abaye (Amora; Critiqued Conflation of Two Cases)
Rav Mesharshiya (Amora; Offered Proofs of Toleh Distinction)
Rava (Amora; Rejected Toleh Distinction; Held All Breira)
Rabbi Yehuda (Tanna; Held Ein Breira for Toleh Bedaat Atzmo)
Rabbi Meir (Tanna; Held Yesh Breira for Wine Separation)
Rabbi Shimon (Tanna; Held Breira for Father's Condition)
Trumot U'Ma'asrot (Priestly Gifts and Tithes)
Tanai (Condition)
Chalitzah (Release from Levirate Marriage)
Kiddushei Biah (Betrothal by Sexual Relations)
